summary: Added a Timeout field to brokeragemap.ExecuteOptions; Execute uses it for the http.Client (falling back to 30s when zero), and every brokerage command threads the CLI --timeout (flags.timeout) into the ExecuteOptions it builds.
reason: Greptile (#893): brokeragemap.Execute hardcoded a 30s http.Client timeout and ExecuteOptions had no Timeout field, so the user-visible --timeout flag (wired into the crypto client via flags.newClient) was silently ignored on every brokerage command. Now brokerage honors --timeout like crypto does.
validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass; verify-skill passes. Default behavior unchanged when --timeout is unset (zero Timeout falls back to 30s).
